vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on Backup gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow-, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow-

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only changes the mode of a single backup file (/etc/gshadow-). Adjusting file permissions is a standard STIG hardening action and does not affect bootloader, partitioning, or critical services. While the file is related to group authentication data, fixing its permissions to 0000 is the recommended secure state and is unlikely to cause lockout or service disruption.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Incorrect permission could expose group password hashes; Changing permissions on auth-related file may affect tools that read the backup
